column3: The event in which an existing material sample is physically divided, producing one or more new sub-samples. The source sample is diminished by the act; each new sub-sample is a full sample entity in its own right.
Document Note: Sample Splitting Model Overview

column1: Document Note: Sample Splitting Model Overview
column3: This model defines a Sample Splitting event (S24), representing the physical act of dividing an existing sample to produce one or more new sub-samples. It captures the source sample, the sub-samples produced, responsible actors, date, location, method, and documentary evidence. The source sample is diminished by the event; each sub-sample produced becomes an independent sample with its own full record. The dual-layer structure (E22: Physical Sample and S13: Material Sample) is carried through from source to sub-samples.
The curated physical artefact being split. The E22 is diminished by the splitting event.
The material sample being split. The S13 is the CRMsci source of the split via O27.
A new curated Physical Sample created as a result of the splitting event, with its own persistent identity, storage, condition, and lifecycle records.

The new material sample produced by the splitting event. Although shown here only in relation to the splitting activity, this sub-sample is a full S13 entity and may independently be observed, measured, modified, and further split.
The actor (individual or organisation) responsible for carrying out or authorising the splitting activity.

column1: //links E39: Organisation or Person --> person, organisation [confirmed]

column3: The physical place where the splitting event occurred (e.g., laboratory, conservation studio).
Any document recording the splitting event, such as treatment notes, laboratory records, or sample management reports.
A defined method or protocol followed during the splitting activity.
Specific tools or equipment used to carry out the split (e.g., scalpel, micro-drill, laser).

column3: Required within some documentation or database systems, such as ResearchSpace required label.

column1: E41: Unique System Label or ID

column3: A brief factual description of the splitting event, including any notable aspects of how the split was performed or the rationale for dividing the sample at this point.
A controlled type or category for the splitting activity (e.g., manual division, micro-sampling, laser ablation).
High-level technique category (e.g., mechanical division, ablation).
The timespan during which the splitting event occurred.
Type or category of tool or equipment used (e.g., scalpel, micro-drill, laser system).
